title: Deglazed German Tempeh Bake
description: A rich and savory German-inspired dish featuring crispy tempeh, caramelized onions, and a tangy deglaze, perfect for a weekend brunch.

ingredients.name: beer
ingredients.notes: any German beer, such as Pilsner or Weissbier

instructions: Crumble the tempeh into small pieces and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the tempeh with 1 tablespoon of olive oil and sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Bake the tempeh in the oven for 20 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Meanwhile, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally, for 15-20 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tempeh from the oven and set aside.
Add the flour to the skillet with the onion mixture and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the beer to the skillet, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the Dijon mustard, apple cider vinegar, and nutritional yeast to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked tempeh to the skillet and toss to coat with the sauce.
Transfer the tempeh mixture to a baking dish and top with fresh parsley.
Bake the tempeh in the oven for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until heated through and the sauce is bubbly.
